mechanism_narrative: The level of industrial production (INDPRO) serves as a proxy for current real economic output. A higher level indicates robust economic activity, which supports corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO level versus subsequent 20-day return is not positive over any rolling 3-year window in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at or below 0.5%, monetary policy is highly accommodative. This compresses discount rates and supports equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The median 20-day forward return in periods with FEDFUNDS <= 0.5 is not significantly greater than zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) over the walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: When both retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) exceed their respective 6-month moving averages, it signals a broad-based demand expansion. This consumer spending impulse lifts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a short 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: In triggered periods, the proportion of positive 5-day returns is below 55% over any continuous 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: In an environment where the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is declining over the past 12 months, a lower current unemployment rate signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer confidence and spending, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When the 12-month UNRATE change is negative, the Spearman rank correlation between the UNRATE level and subsequent 60-day returns is not less than -0.10 (i.e., the expected negative relationship is not observed) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year inflation rate above 2% (as measured by CPIAUCSL) signals rising price pressures that may prompt policy tightening, increasing discount rates and negatively affecting equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where 12-month CPIAUCSL inflation exceeds 2%, the median 20-day return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of low interest rates (FEDFUNDS below 1%) and strong industrial production (INDPRO above 100) provides a dual signal of accommodative policy and real economic strength, which together drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, the proportion of positive 20-day returns is below 55% over any 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: The level of real GDP (GDPC1) represents the overall size of the economy. A higher level implies greater economic output and corporate profitability, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over a longer 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The rolling 5-year Spearman correlation between GDPC1 level and subsequent 60-day returns is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) exceeding 200,000 signals robust job creation, which boosts consumer income and spending, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When payrolls rise more than 200k in a month, the proportion of positive 20-day returns is below 55% over any 3-year walk-forward period.
